description_long: This object is a stylized, compact airplane with a distinctive orange and brown color scheme. It features four prominent cylindrical engines mounted on the wings, which are arranged in an X-shaped configuration, giving it a unique and recognizable silhouette. The fuselage is relatively short and robust, with a forward-placed cockpit that appears to be enclosed. The wings are straight and extend outward with visible support struts and small protrusions at the tips. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is fixed and consists of three visible struts, one at the nose and two at the rear, each ending in a rounded wheel.
description: A compact, orange and brown airplane with four cylindrical engines on X-shaped wings, a forward enclosed cockpit, and fixed tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the four cylindrical engines, X-shaped wings, and the forward cockpit.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the engine mounts, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the engine arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the wing structure, engine placement, and landing gear details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings in an X-shaped configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four visible cylindrical engines mounted on the wings, resembling piston or jet pods.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single visible canopy.
